methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Pano AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Sage Geosystems
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $400M

private.name: Reverion
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $310M

private.name: CarbonCapture Inc.

data.narrative:
Kraftblock is a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Martin Schichtel and Susanne König, having raised €20M+ to date. The valuation analysis relies on a peer-median approach across five same-sector, same-stage private companies: Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita ($120M). This peer set represents the most active comparable companies in early-stage climate tech, supplemented by one public reference point in the sector.

The peer-median method yields a central valuation of $310M, reflecting the median of the five-company sample. The distribution around this point is material: peers range from $120M to $500M, a spread of $380M. To account for this dispersion, the triangulated valuation range extends from $264M (low) to $356M (high), with $310M as the median point. This range assumes Kraftblock's technology maturity, capital efficiency, and market position align with the mid-point of the peer cohort rather than the extremes.

Key uncertainties remain: the limited peer set (five companies) introduces sampling risk, and stage-to-stage calibration across Series B rounds conducted in different market windows may mask valuation drift. The absence of multiple independent methods—reliance on peer-median alone—constrains confidence. A median valuation of $310M is supported by current market comparables, though actual pricing will depend on investor syndicate, revenue trajectory, and category-specific demand at close.
